headlines.title: Uganda’s military chief orders shutdown of two media outlets
headlines.snippet: Uganda's military chief has ordered the shutdown of two media outlets. The president's son expressed disbelief in a free press while military personnel were deployed to the media offices.

headlines.title: ‘US trying to find its way out of MoU with Iran’
headlines.snippet: Bahrain and Kuwait have condemned Iran's retaliatory attacks following a second day of US strikes on Iran. This situation has put a strain on the US-Iran deal.

headlines.title: Iraq security forces arrest several officials in anticorruption crackdown
headlines.snippet: Iraqi security forces have conducted a large-scale operation at dawn in Baghdad's Green Zone and other neighborhoods, arresting several officials as part of an anti-corruption crackdown.

headlines.title: ‘Between pain and fear’: Gaza children bear cost of ongoing Israeli attacks
headlines.snippet: Gaza's children continue to suffer immensely from ongoing Israeli attacks. This suffering persists despite a 'ceasefire' that was reached in October 2025.

headlines.title: Israeli soldier killed in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: An Israeli soldier, identified as 21-year-old David Hazutt, was killed in southern Lebanon. He died near the Lebanese village of Deir Siryan after Israeli forces encountered a Hezbollah operative.

headlines.title: IRGC says it will respond to US attacks 'more strongly than before'

headlines.title: Rights groups criticise Turkey protest ban ahead of Nato summit
